freshness_policy.operator_action: Refresh the row, rerun the named evidence lane, and keep the previous support claim no higher than its rollback status until fresh evidence lands.

support_classes.meaning: A shipped Browser Edition runtime boundary may construct runtime and scope handles directly when the documented browser prerequisites hold.
support_classes.promotion_rule: May remain stable only when package guards, fixture evidence, and browser docs are fresh in the same review window.
support_classes.demotion_rule: Demote to preview or unsupported when runtime construction, package diagnostics, or fixture evidence drift.
no_claims: does not imply native host parity
does not imply service-worker or shared-worker direct runtime support

support_classes.meaning: A repository-maintained wasm/package boundary that feeds the JS/TS Browser Edition packages without becoming an end-user Rust browser SDK by itself.
support_classes.promotion_rule: May feed GA packages when ABI metadata, package files, and compatibility tests stay green.
support_classes.demotion_rule: Demote package release readiness when ABI metadata, package exports, or browser-core fixture checks drift.
no_claims: does not claim a stable external Rust RuntimeBuilder API
does not make browser-core the ergonomic public SDK

support_classes.meaning: A public but narrower Rust-authored browser path with truthful fail-closed diagnostics and fixture-backed evidence.
support_classes.promotion_rule: Cannot promote to stable until builder diagnostics, ABI policy, fixture logs, and docs are promoted together.
support_classes.demotion_rule: Downgrade to internal fixture guidance if the preview builder, docs, or rust-browser-consumer evidence regresses.
no_claims: does not claim parity with the shipped JS/TS packages
does not claim full native-thread runtime semantics on wasm32

support_classes.meaning: The host may coordinate bounded broker or coordinator work, but direct Browser Edition runtime ownership remains fail-closed.
support_classes.promotion_rule: May remain canary or preview only while direct runtime denial is the public truth and broker/coordinator fixtures are fresh.
support_classes.demotion_rule: Remove bounded helper claims if lifecycle, durable handoff, attach, or downgrade evidence regresses.
no_claims: does not promote service-worker or shared-worker direct runtime
does not claim persistent host lifetime parity

support_classes.meaning: The boundary must cross through serialized data, adapter state, or another explicit bridge instead of constructing Browser Edition runtimes directly.
support_classes.promotion_rule: May stay bridge-only when diagnostics and adapter docs prevent direct runtime creation at server or edge boundaries.
support_classes.demotion_rule: Block release if bridge docs or adapter guards imply partial direct-runtime parity.
no_claims: does not tunnel live Browser Edition handles across server or edge boundaries
does not claim direct runtime execution outside browser-owned code

support_classes.meaning: The browser security model or shipped package contract rules out direct Browser Edition support.
support_classes.promotion_rule: No promotion is allowed without a new explicit design, docs, and proof lane.
support_classes.demotion_rule: Keep fail-closed diagnostics and native-runtime or bridge guidance in place.
no_claims: does not claim raw TCP, filesystem, process, signal, or native DB parity
does not hide unsupported browser surfaces behind compatibility shims

required_fixture.validation_command: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_browser_core_package cargo test -p asupersync --test wasm_packaged_abi_compatibility_matrix -- --nocapture

rollback_status.action: Hold Browser Edition package promotion and keep browser-core described as repository ABI infrastructure until metadata and package export evidence is fresh.

promotion_criteria: ABI metadata, package.json exports, TypeScript declarations, and wasm artifacts match the checked package contracts.
Docs keep browser-core scoped as the low-level JS/WASM boundary, not an end-user Rust runtime SDK.
demotion_criteria: ABI fingerprint, package files, or package contract tests drift.
Docs present browser-core as a direct runtime consumer API.
no_claims: not a stable external Rust Browser Edition runtime API
not a replacement for @asupersync/browser

rollback_status.action: Remove stable main-thread runtime language and require browser package diagnostics plus fixture evidence before promotion.

promotion_criteria: Main-thread package diagnostics report supportClass=direct_runtime_supported and reason=supported.
The vanilla/Vite fixture and JS export contract stay green in the same review window.
demotion_criteria: Main-thread detection, WebAssembly prerequisite checks, or fixture bundle/run evidence fails.
Docs imply support outside a browser window/document boundary.
no_claims: does not claim Node, SSR, service-worker, or shared-worker direct runtime
does not claim native filesystem/process/socket parity

rollback_status.action: Keep React usage bridge-only or documentation-only until the client fixture and adapter lifecycle contract are fresh.

promotion_criteria: React package guards admit only client-rendered browser trees.
The React consumer fixture and adapter lifecycle contract remain green.
demotion_criteria: SSR imports, provider lifecycle, or client-boundary diagnostics drift.
Docs imply React SSR direct runtime support.
no_claims: does not support React SSR direct runtime
does not move runtime creation outside a client-rendered browser tree

docs/integration.md: React client-rendered tree in a browser

rollback_status.action: Keep Next guidance bridge-only outside client components until the client fixture and adapter lifecycle contract are fresh.

promotion_criteria: Next client diagnostics admit browser client components only.
The Next fixture validates client bootstrap without widening server or edge boundaries.
demotion_criteria: Client bootstrap, hydration, package guard, or fixture evidence fails.
Docs imply direct runtime in Next server or edge code.
no_claims: does not support direct runtime in Next server components, route handlers, or edge runtimes
does not move live runtime handles across serialization boundaries

rows.display_name: Next server and edge bridge boundaries

rollback_status.action: Block any server or edge direct-runtime release note and keep serialized bridge guidance as the supported path.

promotion_criteria: Bridge adapters return stable unsupported-runtime diagnostics for server and edge targets.
Docs keep direct runtime construction inside client components or browser-only modules.
demotion_criteria: Server or edge helpers import direct runtime entrypoints.
Docs describe server or edge as partial direct-runtime support.
no_claims: no direct Browser Edition runtime execution in Next server code
no direct Browser Edition runtime execution in Next edge code

rollback_status.action: Keep package GA claims from citing vanilla/Vite until bundle and browser-run evidence is fresh.

promotion_criteria: Vite fixture builds, bundles, and runs through the browser harness.
Onboarding receipts keep the vanilla scenario current.
demotion_criteria: Vite bundle, package resolution, or browser-run evidence fails.
Onboarding docs cite stale vanilla artifacts.
no_claims: does not prove every bundler
does not prove unsupported host contexts

rollback_status.action: Do not cite Webpack compatibility for GA signoff until the fixture and bundle checks are fresh.

promotion_criteria: Webpack fixture package resolution and bundle checks pass.
Package exports remain compatible with the Webpack fixture.
demotion_criteria: Webpack package resolution or bundle checks fail.
Docs cite Webpack as fresh without a current fixture lane.
no_claims: does not prove every Webpack plugin or loader combination
does not prove server-side Webpack direct runtime

rollback_status.action: Keep browser main-thread runtime as the stable fallback and demote worker support until worker-specific evidence is fresh.

promotion_criteria: DedicatedWorkerGlobalScope diagnostics admit direct runtime only inside a dedicated worker.
Worker fixture summary and browser-run evidence are fresh.
demotion_criteria: Worker fixture, worker package diagnostics, or worker evidence artifacts fail.
Docs imply automatic upgrade from main thread to worker or service/shared-worker support.
no_claims: does not imply automatic worker offload
does not imply service-worker or shared-worker direct runtime

rollback_status.action: Keep service_worker_not_yet_shipped and service_worker_direct_runtime_not_shipped as the public truth; require main-thread or dedicated-worker fallback guidance.

promotion_criteria: Broker registration, durable handoff, and downgrade diagnostics remain covered by the service-worker fixture.
Direct runtime denial remains explicit in package and Rust diagnostics.
demotion_criteria: Broker lifecycle, durable handoff, summary/browser-run artifacts, or docs drift.
Any docs or package diagnostics imply service-worker direct runtime support.
no_claims: no service-worker direct runtime
no service-worker lifetime or persistence parity beyond the bounded broker contract

rollback_status.action: Keep shared_worker_direct_runtime_not_shipped as the public truth; require main-thread or dedicated-worker fallback guidance.

promotion_criteria: Coordinator attach, version handshake, detach cleanup, and fallback evidence remain fresh.
Direct runtime denial remains explicit in package and Rust diagnostics.
demotion_criteria: Coordinator lifecycle, summary/browser-run artifacts, or docs drift.
Any docs or package diagnostics imply shared-worker direct runtime support.
no_claims: no shared-worker direct runtime
no shared-worker host ownership or lifetime parity

rollback_status.action: Remove preview-public language if RuntimeBuilder::browser diagnostics, docs, or rust-browser-consumer evidence regresses.

promotion_criteria: RuntimeBuilder::browser() keeps truthful lane negotiation and fail-closed diagnostics.
Rust browser consumer fixture and docs remain fresh.
demotion_criteria: Preview builder path widens beyond fixture evidence.
Docs imply stable parity with JS/TS Browser Edition packages.
no_claims: not stable external Rust Browser Edition API parity
not full native-thread runtime parity on wasm32

docs/integration.md: Construct Browser Edition runtimes directly from external Rust consumer code

required_fixture.validation_command: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_browser_abi_artifacts cargo test -p asupersync --test wasm_bundle_size_budget_contract -- --nocapture

rollback_status.action: Block package-level GA signoff until ABI metadata, wasm artifact, and bundle budget evidence are refreshed.

promotion_criteria: ABI metadata, generated JS glue, wasm artifact, declarations, and package files stay internally consistent.
Bundle budget and artifact layout contracts remain green.
demotion_criteria: ABI metadata, wasm artifact, package file, or bundle budget drifts.
Docs cite ABI/package artifacts as fresh without a current contract lane.
no_claims: does not prove every npm publishing or provenance gate
does not prove stable Rust consumer runtime parity

rows.canonical_package: native asupersync or explicit bridge only

required_fixture.fixture_not_required_reason: Unsupported native-only surfaces are guarded by cfg gates, source contracts, and docs; there is no browser-run fixture that should attempt direct raw TCP, filesystem, process, signal, or native DB access.

rollback_status.action: Keep native-only guidance behind native asupersync or explicit bridge code, and reject Browser Edition parity claims.

promotion_criteria: No promotion is allowed without a separate design, docs, source gate, and proof lane.
demotion_criteria: Any docs, package, or adapter code implies raw TCP/UDP, filesystem, process/signal, or native DB direct browser support.
no_claims: no raw TCP or UDP in Browser Edition
no filesystem, process, signal, sqlite, postgres, mysql, kafka, or native TLS direct browser support

validation_commands: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rch_target_browser_readiness_matrix CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' cargo test -p asupersync --test browser_edition_readiness_matrix_contract -- --nocapture
